phrases.french: J'ai réservé une visite des caves.
phrases.english: I have booked a cellar tour.
phrases.phonetic: zhay ray-zair-VAY ewn vee-ZEET day KAV
phrases.context: Taittinger's cellars are former Gallo-Roman chalk quarries and Saint-Nicaise Abbey crypts — some of the most spectacular in Reims. Booking essential.

phrases.french: La dégustation est incluse dans le billet ?
phrases.english: Is the tasting included in the ticket?
phrases.phonetic: lah day-gews-tah-SYON eh an-KLOO don luh bee-YEH
phrases.context: Most cellar tours include a tasting of 1–2 champagnes at the end. Premium tastings with aged vintages cost more.

phrases.english: I would like to buy a bottle.
phrases.context: Buying direct from the house is often slightly cheaper than retail and you can get bottles not widely available elsewhere.
Taittinger is known for its elegant, Chardonnay-dominant style. The Comtes de Champagne blanc de blancs is their prestige cuvée.

vocabulary.english: champagne made entirely from white grapes (Chardonnay)

tips: Book online in advance — Taittinger tours sell out weeks ahead in summer
The cellars extend 18 metres underground into Gallo-Roman chalk quarries — bring a light layer, it's 10°C year-round
Taittinger is one of the few major houses still family-owned — the Taittinger family bought it back in 2006
